frameworks.name: flexible information routing
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial gating of which sensory source feeds the decision circuit

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making (evidence accumulation)
frameworks.one_line: parietal neurons accumulate motion evidence for choice

frameworks.name: selective spatial attention
frameworks.one_line: cued attention selects the informative motion patch

core_question: What neural signature marks the establishment of functional connectivity between sensory and decision circuits when routing is variable across trials?
key_assumptions: parietal cortex carries decision-related signals for motion direction
routing of evidence must be configured before sensory transmission
trial-by-trial uncertainty about source/destination requires an explicit setup signal
manipulations: spatial predictability of the motion stimulus (variable vs fixed location)
presence of an attention cue indicating the informative patch among two
key_findings: transient firing-rate oscillations appeared in parietal neurons at motion onset when location was unpredictable
oscillations also appeared at onset of an attention cue selecting one of two patches
oscillations were absent when stimulus location was fixed across trials
interpretations: the transient oscillation reflects the mechanism that establishes source and destination of routed evidence
the oscillation indexes routing setup rather than the transmission of sensory evidence itself
